mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near zero, additional policy ease has little incremental support for growth and instead reinforces deflation-risk pricing, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 20 trading days as investors buy duration on lower expected nominal growth.
failure_condition: If 10-year Treasury yields rise over the next 20 trading days in more than 60% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL inflation is subdued, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE from an already elevated level signals weakening household income and weaker future demand, which pushes the 10-year Treasury yield lower within 20 trading days through lower growth and inflation expectations.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2pp from the prior month while PAYEMS is flat or declining
failure_condition: If triggered instances do not produce negative 20-day yield changes more often than positive ones, or if the average 20-day yield change is not below zero, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger current spending and firmer nominal demand, which lifts expected inflation and real activity, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS trend signals are followed by negative 20-day yield changes in more than 55% of observations or if the sign relationship flips for two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is weakening while HOUST is also falling, markets infer a broad production slowdown and weaker future inflation pressure, so the 10-year Treasury yield declines over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 3-month moving average and HOUST is down year-over-year
failure_condition: If joint weakness in INDPRO and HOUST is not followed by a negative 20-day yield response in at least 60% of cases, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid CPIAUCSL pickup above recent trend implies higher expected inflation and lower real bond returns, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days as inflation compensation is repriced upward.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates by more than 0.3pp on a 3-month annualized basis
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration triggers are not followed by positive 20-day yield changes at a frequency above 50%, or if the mean 20-day yield change is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDPC1 after a prior expansion signals recession risk and lower future policy rates, which drives the 10-year Treasury yield down over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 slowdowns are not followed by lower 60-day Treasury yields over the next two months more often than not, or if the effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero, reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, simultaneous gains in PAYEMS and declines in UNRATE imply stronger wage pressure and firmer domestic demand, so the 10-year Treasury yield rises over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises for two consecutive months while UNRATE falls by at least 0.1pp
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not produce a positive 20-day yield response in at least 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing-led slowdown, weak HOUST combined with flat-to-falling INDPRO marks a broad cyclical deceleration that lowers expected nominal growth, so the 10-year Treasury yield falls over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below its 6-month average and FEDFUNDS is not rising
failure_condition: If housing weakness under stable policy rates is followed by positive 5-day yield changes more often than negative ones, reject the hypothesis.
